evals.expected_output: An er_diagram.mmd Mermaid erDiagram (or er_diagram_1.mmd/_2.mmd if clustering split it) written next to the input and shown inline in a mermaid fence, plus a short disclosure note naming the source used and entity/relationship counts.
assertions: At least one .mmd file is written next to the input and shown inline in a mermaid code fence
The disclosure note states which source (schema.ir.json) was used
Every relationship line in the diagram references only entities actually declared in it

evals.prompt: draw an ER diagram of this database: design/er-generator/scripts/fixtures/blog.sqlite
evals.expected_output: The skill introspects the SQLite file live via stdlib sqlite3, projects it into the internal ER shape, and renders the same Mermaid output as eval 1's schema-IR path, disclosing that a live SQLite file was the source.
assertions: The disclosure note states the source was a live SQLite file, not a schema IR
No live Postgres/MySQL connection is attempted since none was given
The rendered diagram is shown inline in a mermaid code fence
